title: • Reflection: How often was my mind in the "now" today? What pu
content:
• Reflection: How often was my mind in the "now" today? What pulled it into the past or future?
    • Day 30
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "‘Therefore, it seems to me that everything that exists is good—death as well as life, sin as well as holiness, wisdom as well as folly. Everything is necessary, everything needs only my agreement, my assent, my loving understanding; then all is well with me and nothing can harm me.’"
    • Today's Intention: I will look at every person I meet today and silently acknowledge the universe of experience that exists within them.
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "He was no longer seeing the face of his friend Siddhartha. Instead he saw other faces, many faces... hundreds, thousands, which all came and disappeared and yet all seemed to be there at the same time... and all of them together were Siddhartha."
    • Reflection: In this final reflection, what is the single greatest lesson from this 30-day journey? How will I carry the wisdom of the river forward into my life?
    30-Day Review
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 28 (chunk 28/29)
